tests.source_program:
define i32 @nested_struct_multi_level_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val) {
entry:
  %inner = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val, 1
  %outer = insertvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } poison, { i32, i32 } %inner, 0
  %other = insertvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } poison, i32 42, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %outer, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %other
  %result = extractvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %sel, 0, 1
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @nested_struct_multi_level_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val) {
entry:
  %inner = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val, 1
  %other = insertvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } poison, i32 42, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %outer, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %other
  %result = select i1 %cond, i32 %val, i32 poison
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on single-level extraction indices but this test uses multi-level indices [0, 1] for nested struct aggregates, which the patch handles generically through simplifyExtractValueInst

alive2.src:
define i32 @nested_struct_multi_level_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val) {
entry:
  %inner = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val, 1
  %outer = insertvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } poison, { i32, i32 } %inner, 0
  %other = insertvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } poison, i32 42, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %outer, { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %other
  %result = extractvalue { { i32, i32 }, i32 } %sel, 0, 1
  ret i32 %result
}

define i32 @nested_struct_multi_level_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val) {
entry:
  %inner = insertvalue {i32, i32} poison, i32 %val, 1
  %outer = insertvalue {{i32, i32}, i32} poison, {i32, i32} %inner, 0
  %other = insertvalue {{i32, i32}, i32} poison, i32 42, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, {{i32, i32}, i32} %outer, {{i32, i32}, i32} %other
  %result = extractvalue {{i32, i32}, i32} %sel, 0, 1
  ret i32 %result
}

entry:
  ret i32 %val
}

tests.source_program:
define i64 @array_aggregate_type(i1 %cond, i64 %val) {
entry:
  %arr1 = insertvalue [4 x i64] poison, i64 %val, 2
  %arr2 = insertvalue [4 x i64] poison, i64 100, 2
  %sel = select i1 %cond, [4 x i64] %arr1, [4 x i64] %arr2
  %result = extractvalue [4 x i64] %sel, 2
  ret i64 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i64 @array_aggregate_type(i1 %cond, i64 %val) {
entry:
  %arr1 = insertvalue [4 x i64] poison, i64 %val, 2
  %arr2 = insertvalue [4 x i64] poison, i64 100, 2
  %sel = select i1 %cond, [4 x i64] %arr1, [4 x i64] %arr2
  %result = select i1 %cond, i64 %val, i64 100
  ret i64 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples show struct aggregates but the patch is type-agnostic and also handles array aggregate types, which is not explicitly mentioned in the summary

tests.source_program:
define i32 @insertvalue_both_arms_different_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val1, i32 %val2) {
entry:
  %agg1 = insertvalue { i32, i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val1, 0
  %agg2 = insertvalue { i32, i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val2, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, { i32, i32, i32 } %agg1, { i32, i32, i32 } %agg2
  %result = extractvalue { i32, i32, i32 } %sel, 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @insertvalue_both_arms_different_indices(i1 %cond, i32 %val1, i32 %val2) {
entry:
  %agg1 = insertvalue { i32, i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val1, 0
  %agg2 = insertvalue { i32, i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val2, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, { i32, i32, i32 } %agg1, { i32, i32, i32 } %agg2
  %result = select i1 %cond, i32 %val1, i32 poison
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes extract from insert on one arm with FV as fallback, but this case has insertvalue on BOTH arms with different insertion indices, testing the generic simplifyExtractValueInst path for both branches

alive2.src:

  %agg2 = insertvalue { i32, i32, i32 } poison, i32 %val2, 1

  %result = extractvalue { i32, i32, i32 } %sel, 0

}
